title: Claude Code Agentic OS Is The Future

eval.summary: Generic YouTube pitch for a 'Claude Code Agentic OS' concept combining Claude Code, Obsidian for memory, Google Suite integration, and a sellable dashboard. The video describes the same architecture Veritas has already built (Alan OS, AgentOS, Lux Command Center, VIE pipeline) but at a surface conceptual level with no implementation specifics, no code, no API contracts, no token efficiency data, and no novel techniques. The memory layer uses Obsidian — a GUI-first, non-API-exportable tool that fails P1 and P8 outright. No acceptance-test-level evidence for P3 (token efficiency). The 'skill packs' concept maps loosely to Veritas's vertical config pattern (P5) but adds nothing beyond naming. Content is AI agency hype targeted at people who haven't built what Veritas already runs in production. Zero net-new information.
eval.fit_rationale: Describes an architecture Veritas already operates in production; offers no implementation detail, novel technique, or tool not already evaluated or in-stack.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: nothing — describes what already exists as Alan OS + Lux Command Center + VIE
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P1 because Obsidian is GUI-first with no documented export API path that satisfies the one-hour curl test. Fails P3 because no token efficiency data or eval rubric is provided — content is conceptual only. Fails P7 because every component described (agentic OS engine, memory layer, dashboard, vertical packaging) has ≥90% overlap with existing Veritas stack components, making a BUILD_OR_EXTEND review a foregone rejection. No actionable delta over current production state.
